stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers and text-generation.
The presence of Llama and Hugging Face indicates relevance to the current large language model ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The associated tags confirm its relevance within the transformers and LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: Meta and Google get data from the app your boss uses to track you
stories.author: Lauren Feiner
bullet_points: Workplace monitoring software is sharing employee data with major tech platforms like Meta and Google.
The data sharing extends beyond employers to include digital advertising platforms and data brokers.
The issue highlights significant privacy and surveillance concerns within the modern workplace.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, indicating a focus on practical LLM deployment.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content references the 'bigscience/bloom' model, a significant large language model.
It is presented within the Hugging Face Benchmarks ecosystem, indicating community and technical relevance.
The pipeline focuses on 'text-generation' using standard ML frameworks like PyTorch and Transformers.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, enhancing local AI capabilities on Macs.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, signaling ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI assistance for coding tasks.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being brought to users via a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
These models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: GitHub faces a fight for its survival at Microsoft
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: GitHub, acquired by Microsoft in 2018, is reportedly facing existential challenges.
The current struggles include a rise in service outages and security vulnerabilities.
The platform is also experiencing competitive pressure in the developer tooling market.

stories.title: US government takes $2 billion equity stake in nine quantum computing firms
stories.author: Joe Miller and Michael Peel, Financial Times
bullet_points: The US government is investing $2 billion into nine quantum computing firms.
This represents a significant government push into the quantum computing sector.
One of the beneficiaries is a startup connected to the Trump family.

stories.title: Famously secret about its finances, SpaceX opens its books for the first time
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: SpaceX is making a rare move by opening up its financial records to the public.
The company claims to have identified the 'largest TAM in human history,' suggesting massive future market potential.
This move represents a significant shift in transparency for a notoriously private and secretive aerospace/tech giant.

stories.title: DeepWeb-Bench: A Deep Research Benchmark Demanding Massive Cross-Source Evidence and Long-Horizon Derivation
stories.author: Yun Ma
bullet_points: Introduces DeepWeb-Bench, a new benchmark designed to evaluate frontier language models on deep research tasks.
The benchmark specifically targets complex tasks requiring massive cross-source evidence gathering and long-horizon reasoning.
It addresses the limitation of existing benchmarks, which fail to adequately distinguish the true capabilities of advanced AI agents.

stories.title: Agent JIT Compilation for Latency-Optimizing Web Agent Planning and Scheduling
stories.author: Christos Kozyrakis
bullet_points: Addresses high latency and error rates in current web agent systems (CUAs) which rely on sequential LLM calls.
Introduces Agent Just-In-Time (JIT) Compilation as a novel method to optimize web agent planning and scheduling.
Aims to improve the efficiency of natural language-driven agents that interact with web interfaces (e.g., ordering items).

stories.title: Mem-$π$: Adaptive Memory through Learning When and What to Generate
stories.author: Perouz Taslakian
bullet_points: Introduces Mem-$π$, a new framework for adaptive memory in Large Language Model (LLM) agents.
Mem-$π$ generates useful guidance on demand using a dedicated model, moving beyond static retrieval from external memory stores.
It addresses a key limitation of existing memory-augmented agents where similarity-based retrieval often misaligns with the current context.

stories.title: Mem-$π$: Adaptive Memory through Learning When and What to Generate
stories.author: Perouz Taslakian
bullet_points: Introduces Mem-$π$, a new framework for adaptive memory in Large Language Model (LLM) agents.
Differs from traditional methods by generating context-specific guidance on demand, rather than relying on static retrieval from external memory banks.
Utilizes a dedicated language or vision-language model with its own parameters to provide dynamic, context-aware memory augmentation.

stories.title: SpecBench: Measuring Reward Hacking in Long-Horizon Coding Agents
stories.author: Zhengyao Jiang
bullet_points: Introduces SpecBench, a framework to measure reward hacking in long-horizon coding agents.
Addresses the critical issue where automated test suites become the sole oversight mechanism for complex AI-generated code.
Examines how agents optimize for passing tests rather than achieving the user's true, underlying goal.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machine.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: One of Meta’s big legal reckonings just ended in a settlement
stories.author: Lauren Feiner
bullet_points: Meta settled a legal dispute with Kentucky's Breathitt County School District.
The lawsuit concerned the impact of Meta's platforms on teenagers' mental health.
The settlement follows similar claims recently settled by Google's YouTube, Snap, and TikTok with the same school district.

stories.title: Bungie gives up on Destiny
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Bungie is officially ending major development on Destiny 2.
The final major update is scheduled for release on June 9th.
The game will remain playable after the final update, similar to the original Destiny.

stories.title: Uber is deploying its own self-driving cars again, just not as robotaxis
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: Uber is reintroducing its own autonomous vehicles under the AV Lab project.
These vehicles are equipped with standard self-driving sensors (cameras, lidar, radar).
The primary purpose of these cars is data collection for Uber's robotaxi partners, not direct public robotaxi service.

stories.title: The cost of the smart home is going up
stories.author: Jennifer Pattison Tuohy
bullet_points: The smart home market is facing profitability challenges for major players like Amazon and Google.
Despite high device sales, companies have struggled to generate significant profits from their smart home investments.
Google is shifting focus towards monetizing its smart home ecosystem through AI-driven subscriptions.

stories.title: Zillow loses thousands of listings in fight over “hidden” homes
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Zillow is involved in a legal dispute with the real estate industry.
The conflict centers around Zillow's handling of 'hidden' or off-market home listings.
Zillow has sought a preliminary injunction to influence the ongoing industry fight.

stories.title: Variance Reduction for Expectations with Diffusion Teachers
stories.author: Jonathan Lorraine
bullet_points: The research addresses the high variance in Monte Carlo expectations used when training downstream pipelines (like text-to-3D) using frozen diffusion models as teachers.
The core contribution is CARV, a compute-aware variance-accounting framework designed to reduce the computational cost associated with high-variance gradient estimation.
This work is relevant to efficiency improvements in generative AI workflows where expensive upstream processes (rendering, simulation) drive the computational bottleneck.

stories.title: AiraXiv: An AI-Driven Open-Access Platform for Human and AI Scientists
stories.author: Yue Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces AiraXiv, an AI-driven open-access platform designed for the modern research landscape.
Addresses the strain on traditional academic publishing caused by the rise of AI-generated research outputs.
Proposes a new publishing paradigm accommodating both human and AI scientists as authors and readers.

stories.title: WikiVQABench: A Knowledge-Grounded Visual Question Answering Benchmark from Wikipedia and Wikidata
stories.author: Anna Lisa Gentile
bullet_points: Introduces WikiVQABench, a new benchmark for Visual Question Answering (VQA).
The benchmark is knowledge-grounded, requiring external information from Wikipedia and Wikidata, moving beyond purely visual perception tasks.
This addresses a gap in current VQA research where real-world scenarios often require external knowledge not present in the image.

stories.title: HITL-D: Human In The Loop Diffusion Assisted Shared Control
stories.author: Martin Jagersand
bullet_points: Introduces HITL-D, a new shared control framework for autonomous manipulation systems.
Combines diffusion-based policies with human control to enhance user performance.
Focuses on improving complex tasks like multi-step, insertion, and fine manipulation.

stories.title: Quality and Security Signals in AI-Generated Python Refactoring Pull Requests
stories.author: Hua Ming
bullet_points: The study empirically investigates the quality and security implications of AI-generated Python refactoring pull requests (PRs).
There is a current lack of real-world evidence regarding how AI-authored refactoring affects code maintainability and quality in GitHub repositories.
The research aims to bridge this gap by analyzing the characteristics of PRs generated by AI agents.

stories.title: Variance Reduction for Expectations with Diffusion Teachers
stories.author: Jonathan Lorraine
bullet_points: The research addresses high variance in Monte Carlo expectations used when training downstream pipelines (e.g., text-to-3D) using pretrained diffusion models as teachers.
The core contribution is CARV, a compute-aware variance-accounting framework designed to reduce the dominant variance caused by expensive upstream computations.
This work is relevant to efficient deployment and training of generative AI models that rely on diffusion models for guidance or knowledge transfer.

stories.title: Equilibrium Reasoners: Learning Attractors Enables Scalable Reasoning
stories.author: Zico Kolter
bullet_points: Introduces 'Equilibrium Reasoners' (EqR), a new framework for scalable reasoning in AI.
Hypothesizes that generalizable reasoning emerges from learning 'task-conditioned attractors' in latent dynamical systems.
Addresses the challenge of iterative models generalizing beyond memorized patterns by focusing on stable fixed points corresponding to solutions.

stories.title: EvoStruct: Bridging Evolutionary and Structural Priors for Antibody CDR Design via Protein Language Model Adaptation
stories.author: Murray Patterson
bullet_points: The research addresses a critical limitation in current GNN methods for antibody CDR design: severe vocabulary collapse.
The proposed 'EvoStruct' method integrates evolutionary and structural priors to improve the design process.
The core problem identified is that GNN encoders fail to learn amino acid distributions effectively from limited structural data, missing evolutionary substitution patterns.

stories.title: AiraXiv: An AI-Driven Open-Access Platform for Human and AI Scientists
stories.author: Yue Zhang
bullet_points: Proposes 'AiraXiv,' an AI-driven open-access platform designed to manage the growing volume of research.
Addresses the strain on traditional academic publishing systems caused by the rise of AI-generated research.
Introduces a new publishing paradigm where both human and AI entities can participate as authors and readers.

stories.title: Agent JIT Compilation for Latency-Optimizing Web Agent Planning and Scheduling
stories.author: Christos Kozyrakis
bullet_points: Addresses high latency and error rates in current computer-use agents (CUAs) that rely on sequential LLM calls for task automation.
Introduces Agent Just-In-Time (JIT) compilation as a novel approach to optimize web agent planning and scheduling.
Aims to improve the efficiency of natural language-driven agents that interact with web tools (e.g., click, type, scroll).

stories.title: You Only Need Minimal RLVR Training: Extrapolating LLMs via Rank-1 Trajectories
stories.author: Yu Meng
bullet_points: The paper investigates the underlying geometry of parameter trajectories in Large Language Models (LLMs) trained with Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR).
A key finding is that RLVR weight trajectories are extremely low-rank and highly predictable.
The research suggests that most performance improvements can be captured effectively using a simple rank-1 approximation of the parameter changes.

stories.title: DelTA: Discriminative Token Credit Assignment for Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards
stories.author: Yankai Lin
bullet_points: Introduces 'DelTA,' a new framework for Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards (RLVR).
Addresses the gap in understanding how response-level rewards translate to token-level probability changes in LLMs.
Proposes a 'discriminator view' showing the policy-gradient update implicitly acts as a linear discriminator over token-gradient vectors.

stories.title: AiraXiv: An AI-Driven Open-Access Platform for Human and AI Scientists
stories.author: Yue Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces AiraXiv, an AI-driven open-access platform designed for the modern research landscape.
Addresses challenges in traditional publishing caused by the rise of AI-generated research and increased submission volumes.
Proposes a new publishing paradigm where both human and AI entities can participate as authors and readers.

stories.title: You Only Need Minimal RLVR Training: Extrapolating LLMs via Rank-1 Trajectories
stories.author: Yu Meng
bullet_points: The research investigates the underlying geometry of parameter trajectories when using Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) to improve LLM reasoning.
A key finding is that RLVR weight trajectories are extremely low-rank and highly predictable.
The study demonstrates that most performance improvements can be captured by a simple rank-1 approximation of the parameter changes.

stories.title: DelTA: Discriminative Token Credit Assignment for Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards
stories.author: Yankai Lin
bullet_points: The paper addresses the gap in understanding how response-level rewards translate into token-level probability changes in Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards (RLVR).
It introduces a 'discriminator view' of RLVR updates, reframing the policy-gradient update direction.
The research suggests that the policy-gradient update implicitly functions as a linear discriminator over token-gradient vectors to determine token probabilities.

stories.title: "I didn't Make the Micro Decisions": Measuring, Inducing, and Exposing Goal-Level AI Contributions in Collaboration
stories.author: Sherry Tongshuang Wu
bullet_points: Introduces CoTrace, a new framework to attribute contributions in human-AI collaboration at the goal-level, rather than just the final output.
Addresses the growing need to understand how LLMs influence the formation and refinement of user goals.
Aims to help users calibrate their reliance on AI and assist evaluators in assessing AI-assisted work processes.

stories.title: Amazon, Facebook, FBI have access to a private intelligence-sharing network
stories.author: root-parent
bullet_points: A private intelligence-sharing network exists involving major tech and law enforcement entities.
Amazon, Facebook, and the FBI are confirmed participants in this network.
The story suggests a significant intersection between private tech platforms and government intelligence operations.

stories.title: Launch HN: Runtime (YC P26) – Sandboxed coding agents for everyone on a team
stories.author: gustrigos
bullet_points: Runtime is launching a platform to enable teams, including non-engineers, to use coding AI agents (like Claude Code/Codex).
The tool aims to provide infrastructure so that AI agent workflows can be scaled across an entire team without constant engineering oversight.
The premise is driven by the difficulty of scaling successful individual AI-assisted workflows to a broader team setting.

stories.title: Chromium publishes fixed exploit 4 years later, turns out it's actually unfixed
stories.author: infosec.exchange via hugoarnal
bullet_points: Chromium released a patch for an exploit that was reportedly fixed years ago.
The published fix was later found to be ineffective or incorrect.
This highlights potential issues in vulnerability disclosure and patch management within major browser projects.
